bibliographic_data.title: An outbreak of Salmonella enterica serovar Othmarschen at a funeral service in Guri-si, South Korea.
bibliographic_data.abstract: Salmonella is one of the major contagious enteric pathogens regularly implicated in cases of food-borne disease in Korea. The most frequent serovars isolated from human beings in Korea were Salmonella enterica serovar Enteritidis, Salmonella enterica serovar Typhimurium, and Salmonella enterica serovar Typhi. However, Salmonella enterica serovar Othmarschen is a relatively rare serovar which has been isolated from humans as well as animals in Korea. In this report, we describe a significant outbreak of S. Othmarschen, which resulted in severe salmonellosis among persons attending a funeral service in Guri-si.

core_content.novelty_claim: Describes a significant outbreak of Salmonella enterica serovar Othmarschen at a funeral service in South Korea, highlighting the severe health impact and the need for better food safety measures.
core_content.key_methodology: The team conducted interviews, collected specimens from patients and food handlers, and evaluated the specimens for pathogens. Microbiological examinations were then conducted on the specimens to identify the source of the outbreak.
outcomes: S. Othmarschen was isolated from 3 samples of remaining food, a food handler, and 35 patients
All isolates were found to be susceptible to 16 antibiotics employed in their laboratory
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper describes an outbreak of Salmonella enterica serovar Othmarschen at a funeral service in Guri-si, South Korea. The outbreak resulted in severe salmonellosis among persons attending the event. The authors conducted interviews and collected specimens from patients and food handlers to identify the source of the infection. Microbiological examinations revealed that S. Othmarschen was isolated from 3 samples of remaining food, a food handler, and 35 patients. All isolates were found to be susceptible to 16 antibiotics employed in their laboratory. The study highlights the need for better food safety measures, particularly with regard to chicken and egg products.
